## Task
Review miss from REGISTRY-AUTODISCOVER-001 (found 2026-07-10 minutes after merge): the auto-discovery test's fixture command lives at src/cli/commands/__fixture__.ts — INSIDE the scanned directory — so the PRODUCTION CLI ships and lists a `__fixture__` command in its help. Test fixtures must never leak into the shipped command surface.
1. Move the fixture out of the scanned directory (test fixtures under the test tree or a fixtures dir the generator explicitly ignores) OR make the generator exclude a documented test-only convention — ONE rule, stated in the generator, not ad-hoc.
2. Assert the shipped surface: a test that builds the real registry and asserts NO command name matches the test-only convention (and specifically that __fixture__ is absent from help output).
3. Red-team case: dropping a stray file in src/cli/commands/ that does not export a valid descriptor must fail the layout check (verify), never silently ship.

## RED test (write first)
In src/cli/registry.test.ts add a test named exactly: "the production registry excludes test-only fixture commands". Build the registry and assert no __fixture__ (or convention-matching) command is present. Today it IS present -> it FAILS.
Expected failure cause (literal string in the output): the test name "the production registry excludes test-only fixture commands".

## Reuse
The generated-index generator from REGISTRY-AUTODISCOVER-001; the layout test; RAIL-REDTEAM-001's suite for the stray-file case.

## Stop conditions
An exclusion hack for one filename instead of a stated convention; weakening the auto-discovery test (it must still prove discovery works, against a fixture OUTSIDE the shipped scan); touching files outside the write_set.

## Evidence required at close
red-test-output, verify-root, final-sha.
